free_text: Nonstatistical gamma emission in 3He- and alpha-
A 10x10 in. cylindrical NaI crystal surrounded
by an NE 110 plastic anticoincidence shield and by
passive 6LiH and paraffin neutron shielding, with
Targets were self-supported rolled foils of the
All targets were isotopically enriched to at least 90%
except for 119Sn, which was enriched to 86%.
Absolute normalization of spectra was made using cross
section from 2+ to 0+ transition in 112 Sn.
Spectra were corrected for contribution of target
impurities. The largest correction for impurities was
for the Sm targets; for a1pha 148Sm, 2% corrections
were made for 15 MeV <= Eg <=20 MeV, and 10%
were 15% above Eg=20 MeV. For all other targets the
corrections were 1% or less for gamma-ray energies at
or above the giant dipole resonance energy.
No information on source of uncertainties.
